description: Policy is the Schema for the Tinkerbell Policy API.

apiVersion.description:
APIVersion defines the versioned schema of this representation of an object.
Servers should convert recognized schemas to the latest internal value, and
may reject unrecognized values.
More info: https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/devel/sig-architecture/api-conventions.md#resources

kind.description:
Kind is a string value representing the REST resource this object represents.
Servers may infer this from the endpoint the client submits requests to.
Cannot be updated.
In CamelCase.
More info: https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/devel/sig-architecture/api-conventions.md#types-kinds

spec.description: PolicySpec defines the desired state of all Policies.

rules.description: Rules defines the different types of rules that are applied for a Policy.

referenceAccess.description: ReferenceAccess defines rules for granting access to References based on object pattern matching.

hardware.description:
Hardware defines rules for granting Hardware objects access to References.
Multiple rules in this array are combined with OR logic (any rule can match).
Within each rule, fields are combined with AND logic (all specified fields must match).
If any rule matches, the Hardware templating will have access to the Reference object(s) through the corresponding Hardware object.

items.description:
AccessRule represents a Quamina pattern for matching reference access events.
Multiple fields within a single rule are combined with AND logic.
Use an array of AccessRule to combine multiple rules with OR logic.

reference.description: Reference matches the object being referenced.

group.description:
Group matches the API group of the referenced object.
Multiple values are combined with OR logic.

items.description:
PatternValue can hold a string, number, or boolean for exact matching.
PatternValue represents a Quamina pattern value.
It can be a plain value (string, number, boolean) for exact match,
or an object with pattern operators like prefix, suffix, wildcard, etc.
Examples:
  - "value" (exact match string)
  - 123 (exact match number)
  - true (exact match boolean)

name.description:
Name matches the name of the referenced object.
Multiple values are combined with OR logic.

task.description:
Task defines rules for granting Task objects access to References.
Multiple rules in this array are combined with OR logic (any rule can match).
Within each rule, fields are combined with AND logic (all specified fields must match).
If any rule matches, the Task templating will have access to the Reference object(s) through the corresponding Task object.

Multiple fields within a single rule are combined with AND logic.
Use an array of AccessRule to combine multiple rules with OR logic.

x-kubernetes-validations.message: at least one of source or reference must be specified

workflowAutoCreation.description:
WorkflowAutoCreation defines rules for automatically creating Workflows based on Agent attributes.
Multiple rules in this array are combined with OR logic (any rule can match).
Within each rule, fields are combined with AND logic (all specified fields must match).
If any rule matches, the corresponding Workflow will be created.

items.description: WorkflowRule defines the Rules, options, and Workflow to be created on rules match.

config.description: Config holds the configuration used to create the Workflow when a rule matches.

addAttributes.description: AddAttributes indicates if the Agent attributes should be added as an Annotation in the created Workflow.

disabled.description: Disabled indicates whether the Workflow will be enabled or not when created.

globals.description:
Globals holds any additional configuration values that will be applied to all Tasks in the created Workflow.
This maps to WorkflowSpec.Globals.

envVars.description: EnvVars defined here are additive to any existing environment variables.

items.description: EnvVar defines a single environment variable.

key.description: Key is the name of the environment variable.

value.description: Value is the value of the environment variable.

templateMap.description: TemplateMap is a mapping of key/values that will be used when templating a Task.

volumes.description: Volumes defined here are additive to any existing volumes.

items.description:
Volume is a specification for mounting a location on a Host into an Action container.
Volumes take the form {SRC-VOLUME-NAME | SRC-HOST-DIR}:TGT-CONTAINER-DIR:OPTIONS.
When specifying a VOLUME-NAME that does not exist it will be created for you.
Examples:

Read-only bind mount bound to /data

	/etc/data:/data:ro

Writable volume name bound to /data

	shared_volume:/data

See https://docs.docker.com/storage/volumes/ for additional details.

namespace.description: Namespace is the namespace in which the Workflow will be created.

tasks.description: Tasks defines the Tasks created in the Workflow.

items.description: PolicyWorkflowTask defines a Task entry for auto-created Workflows.

extra.description: Extra configuration that is applied to this specific Task.

envVars.description: EnvVars defined here are additive to any existing environment variables.

items.description: EnvVar defines a single environment variable.

key.description: Key is the name of the environment variable.

value.description: Value is the value of the environment variable.

templateMap.description: TemplateMap is a mapping of key/values that will be used when templating a Task.

volumes.description: Volumes defined here are additive to any existing volumes.

items.description:
Volume is a specification for mounting a location on a Host into an Action container.
Volumes take the form {SRC-VOLUME-NAME | SRC-HOST-DIR}:TGT-CONTAINER-DIR:OPTIONS.
When specifying a VOLUME-NAME that does not exist it will be created for you.
Examples:

Read-only bind mount bound to /data

	/etc/data:/data:ro

Writable volume name bound to /data

	shared_volume:/data

See https://docs.docker.com/storage/volumes/ for additional details.

taskRef.description: TaskRef is the Task to run.

x-kubernetes-validations.message: name and namespace must both be specified or both be empty

timeoutSeconds.description:
TimeoutSeconds is the duration before a Task time out is reached.
A zero or nil value means no timeout.
TimeoutSeconds is the duration in seconds before the Workflow times out.

rules.description:
Rules is a list of Quamina patterns used to match against the attributes of an Agent.
See https://github.com/timbray/quamina/blob/main/PATTERNS.md for more information on the required format.
Multiple rules in this array are combined with OR logic (any rule can match).
Within each rule, fields are combined with AND logic (all specified fields must match).
If any rule matches, the corresponding Workflow will be created.

items.description:
AgentAttributes is a Quamina pattern that matches against Agent attributes.
Multiple fields within a single AgentAttributes are combined with AND logic (all specified fields must match).
Multiple values within each FieldPattern field are combined with OR logic (any value can match).

product.description:
FieldPattern represents all possible pattern values for a single field.
Multiple values in the array are combined with OR logic (any value can match).

items.description:
PatternValue can hold a string, number, or boolean for exact matching.
PatternValue represents a Quamina pattern value.
It can be a plain value (string, number, boolean) for exact match,
or an object with pattern operators like prefix, suffix, wildcard, etc.
Examples:
  - "value" (exact match string)
  - 123 (exact match number)
  - true (exact match boolean)

PatternValue can hold a string, number, or boolean for exact matching.
PatternValue represents a Quamina pattern value.
It can be a plain value (string, number, boolean) for exact match,
or an object with pattern operators like prefix, suffix, wildcard, etc.
Examples:
  - "value" (exact match string)
  - 123 (exact match number)
  - true (exact match boolean)
